exampleSentences: Chị luôn giữ nhà cửa sạch để cảm thấy dễ chịu hơn.
Em rửa tay sạch trước khi ăn cơm với gia đình.
Anh thích mặc áo quần sạch mỗi ngày đi làm.
notes:
sạch = clean, not dirty. It describes things like rooms, clothes, hands, food, or water.

As an adjective, it usually comes after the noun: nhà sạch, tay sạch. You can add degree words like rất sạch, quá sạch, không sạch.

It also has a very common meaning of “completely / totally” when used after a verb.  

Strong contrast: sạch (clean) vs bẩn (dirty).
notes_md:
**sạch** = clean, not dirty. It describes things like rooms, clothes, hands, food, or water.

As an adjective, it usually comes after the noun: *nhà sạch, tay sạch*. You can add degree words like **rất sạch**, **quá sạch**, **không sạch**.

It also has a very common meaning of “completely / totally” when used after a verb.  

Strong contrast: **sạch** (clean) vs **bẩn** (dirty).

translation.en: This dining table is very clean.

exampleSentencesTranslated: She always keeps the house clean to feel more comfortable.
I wash my hands clean before eating with my family.
He likes to wear clean clothes every day to work.

definitions.en: Free from dirt, marks, or stains.
definitions.es: Libre de suciedad, marcas o manchas.
definitions.vi: Không có bụi bẩn, vết bẩn hoặc vết ố.
